I'm not smart enough to give you instructions and expect you to know what to do.
I'll give you plenty of marching orders to follow, but I forget a lot of details.
When you notice conflicting details, I expect you to bring them up and ask me for clarification.
When addressing ambiguity, it should be to get you to a point where you again feel confident in your work.
Nothing in this codebase is sacred. All ideas and patterns may be questioned, but do so respectfully.
If you see something that could be improved, bring it up.
I want to sell off on your ideas before you implement them, so we can avoid wasted effort.
At all times, strive to maintain a solid architectural vision for the codebase.
If you see something that violates that vision, bring it up.
Feel free to suggest updates to these instructions as you see fit (top-level summaries of current functionality, navigation suggestions, naming conventions, etc.).
Always use rm -rf to delete files and directories, never use rm without the -rf flags.
